investigation_findings.current_state: src/runtime/components/CommandBar.tsx:49 — isLocalOnlyCommand returns `kind === 'select' || kind === 'measure' || kind === 'snapshot'`. The production code is correct: a `snapshot` (or valid `measure ... to ...`) command typed in the CommandBar correctly returns true and gets its single recall-supporting history entry.
investigation_findings.gap: No test submits a valid `snapshot`-kind or valid `measure e_N to e_M`-kind command through the CommandBar Enter path. controls.test.tsx submits only `select e_1` (exercises the 1st disjunct) and `measure e_2 e_3` — the latter is invalid grammar (RE_MEASURE requires the `to` keyword) so parseCommand throws and isLocalOnlyCommand returns true via its `catch` branch, never reaching L49's `measure`/`snapshot` disjuncts. The `measure`/`snapshot` disjuncts of L49 are therefore unexercised, leaving the R18 `or-to-and` mutant (`select || (measure && snapshot)`, which reduces to `select`-only) alive. This is a benign coverage margin, NOT a behavioral gap, dead path, or swallowed failure — the shipping code path is correct and matches SPEC §8.6/§9.4. Recorded P3/SUSPECTED so the planner can decide whether to close the margin with a `snapshot foo` + `measure e_2 to e_3` CommandBar Enter test; it does not block convergence.
investigation_findings.re_read: Empirically reproduced: `sed` injected the R18 `or-to-and` mutant into CommandBar.tsx:49, ran `npx vitest run tests/unit/runtime/controls.test.tsx tests/unit/runtime/history-persist-ownership.test.tsx` -> 17/17 still passed (mutant survives), then restored the file. Confirmed source: `grep -n 'snapshot\|measure\|select' tests/unit/runtime/controls.test.tsx` -> only `select e_1` (L184,L237) and `measure e_2 e_3` (L260, invalid grammar) are ever submitted. `grep -n 'RE_MEASURE' src/runtime/parsers/operation-parser.ts` -> `/^measure\s+(e_\d+)\s+to\s+(e_\d+)$/i` confirms `measure e_2 e_3` does not parse.

axes_swept: 1 — Build pipeline (transformJSX data-pin injection, enabled/filePattern/excludePattern gating, excludeTags + data-pin-ignore honored)
2 — Pin-ID stability (stableKey file:line:column, getOrAssign monotonic, reconcile soft-delete no-reuse)
3 — Production-zero (transformIndexHtml stripPins; AC-010/AC-074 build-grep over dist returns 0)
4 — Runtime isolation (createPortal to document.body under data-pinscope-ui=root, z-index 2147483645-647)
5 — Inspection layer (useHoveredElement nearest [data-pin] ancestor, InfoPanel sections, PinBadges/VoidBadges)
6 — Measurement layer (Rulers, Crosshair, GridOverlay 4 modes, MeasurementTool dx/dy/diagonal/gap)
7 — Operation protocol (parseCommand all SPEC §11 grammar forms + typed OperationParseError; CommandBar Enter path)
8 — Data schemas (Operation/Snapshot/History types vs §9; 32 TRACKED_STYLES verified; history persist body {version,entries})
9 — Edge cases (iframe-overlay, shadow-dom, throttle, long-press, rect-math, screenshot utils all present)
10 — Performance budgets (AC-070/071/072/073/074/075/076 all PASS; html2canvas dynamic import verified)
11 — Integration surface (Vite plugin functional; package.json exports map; Next/Webpack scoped P4/P5 per §18 I-1)
12 — Phase DoD (P1-P5 DoD ACs all PASS or BLOCKED-by-env)
notes: withPinScope (next.ts) and PinScopeWebpackPlugin (webpack.ts) carry explicit 'a later round registers the data-pin loader' comments and do not yet inject data-pin. This is NOT an off-matrix finding: §18 resolution I-1 explicitly scopes the Next.js wrapper and Webpack plugin as P4/P5, and the only AC naming them, AC-092, requires solely that they 'are importable and return valid config objects' — which they do (AC-092 PASS). The frozen spec does not require functional Next/Webpack injection at north-star v2.0; the 'later round' comments match the spec's phased intent. Recorded here for round-over-round blind-spot visibility, not as a finding.

package.json size-limit config has a single entry (runtime dev, 80 KB) and lacks discrete <25 KB gzip / <30 KB P1 sub-budget / prod 0-byte entries that §13's prose table enumerates. Not an off-matrix finding: AC-073 verify is `npm run size` exit 0 and AC-074 verify is the dist build-grep (AC-074 explicitly 'covered by AC-010 at artifact level') — both PASS. §13 is non-normative prose; AC-073/074 are the normative contract and they pass.
